methods.description: Process for extracting and preparing climate and biome data for analysis over the past 800,000 years.

steps.description: Mean annual temperature (bio01), total annual precipitation (bio12) and biome (biomeoutput04) were extracted from the model for 1000 year time slices and stored in a raster stack using the 'raster' R package.
steps.searchable_summary: extract climate and biome data for 1000-year time slices using 'raster' R package
outputs: raster stack of climate and biome data

steps.description: Selected the time slices corresponding to the mid-age of each assemblage, rounded to the nearest 1000 years to match the resolution of the climate data.
steps.searchable_summary: select and round time slices to match climate data resolution

steps.description: Each time slice was cropped according to the predicted sea level for that period, using global sea-level change data and a bathymetry model.
steps.searchable_summary: crop time slices using sea-level change data and bathymetry model

steps.description: Integrated the climate model with an archaeological dataset comprising presence/absence data of tool types and raw material types for eastern African MSA assemblages.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate climate model with archaeological dataset

methods.description: Refinement of climatic parameters by extracting and downscaling temperature and precipitation values for analysis.

steps.description: Extracted temperature and precipitation values in 50 km radius buffers around the coordinates of each occupation from its mid-age time slice.
steps.searchable_summary: extract temperature and precipitation values in 50 km buffers

steps.description: Downscaled model datasets from 30' to 2.5' by resampling the data using bilinear interpolation.
Calculated the mean and standard deviation of the values across the 50 km buffer for each occupation.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean and standard deviation for buffer values

methods.description: Cluster analysis to examine grouping of occupations based on temperature and precipitation data.

steps.description: Calculated dissimilarity matrices for the temperature and precipitation data using the Euclidean distance.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate dissimilarity matrices using Euclidean distance

steps.description: Hierarchical clustering of the dissimilarity matrices was employed to examine grouping of occupations.
steps.searchable_summary: perform hierarchical clustering on dissimilarity matrices

steps.description: Applied the average silhouette method using k-means clustering to determine the optimal number of clusters.
steps.searchable_summary: use k-means clustering to determine optimal number of clusters

steps.description: Created a dendrogram of the occupations and cut it according to the k-means output to define clusters.
steps.searchable_summary: create and cut dendrogram based on k-means output
